name: Coconut-Mango Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 4 packages Cream cheese (8 ounce), at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the Pan and Ingredients: 15-oz Can Cream of Coconut from Coco Lopez (available in the liquor department of most supermarkets). Halve, pit, peel, and slice the fresh mangoes.
For the Crust: Wrap the outside of a 9-inch-diameter springform pan with foil. Mix graham cracker crumbs, toasted shredded coconut, and sugar in a medium bowl. Add melted butter and mix to blend. Press mixture into the bottom and up the sides of the prepared pan. Chill while preparing the filling.
For the Filling: Beat cream cheese and 3/4 cup sugar in a large bowl until blended. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition. Beat in egg yolk. Add canned coconut, whipping cream, and shredded coconut; beat just until blended. Pour into crust.
Preheat Oven to 325°F (165°C). Bake cheesecake until puffed and golden, about 1 hour and 25 minutes. Transfer to a rack and cool completely. Refrigerate until well chilled (can be made up to one day ahead. Cover and keep refrigerated).
Puree 1/4 of the mango slices in a blender until smooth. Sweeten to taste with sugar if desired. Using a small knife, cut around the cheesecake to loosen it. Remove pan sides. Arrange remaining mango slices decoratively over the cheesecake. Serve with mango puree.
